analysis: Hotspot is MPI wait/synchronization from many small blocking sends/recvs plus frequent barriers. Apply the selected Non-Blocking I/O pattern by posting per-chunk Irecv/Isend requests with the same tags, then completing them with a single MPI_Waitall per iteration. This preserves message matching/order via tags and avoids buffer reuse until completion. Intra-chunk and per-iteration barriers used for synchronization are removed; only timing barriers are kept.
selected_candidate_target: MPI communication synchronization overhead (excessive blocking + barriers)
applied_changes_summary: Replaced per-chunk blocking MPI_Send/MPI_Recv and surrounding MPI_Barrier calls with non-blocking MPI_Irecv/MPI_Isend for each chunk and a single MPI_Waitall per iteration. Removed intra-chunk and per-iteration synchronization barriers, retaining only the outer barriers around timing.
correctness_risks: Incorrect tag/source pairing could cause deadlock or mismatched messages if modified incorrectly (kept identical tags 1000+c and 2000+c and fixed peer ranks).
Buffer region reuse before request completion could corrupt data (avoided by waiting for all requests each iteration before continuing).
expected_metrics: Lower TIME_SEC by reducing synchronization/latency overhead from many barriers and blocking calls
Reduced mpi_wait_pct due to increased overlap and fewer global synchronization points
